full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What sklearn dataset would work well for testing a multiclass SVM method? I need at least 100 samples, 2+ features, and 3+ classes to see the ℓ₂,∞ vs Frobenius difference
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI suggested several options: load_digits (1797 samples, 64 features, 10 classes), load_wine (178 samples, 13 features, 3 classes), and load_iris (150 samples, 4 features, 3 classes). It recommended load_digits as the best fit for multiclass SVM testing since it has many classes and enough samples for meaningful train/test splits.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Suggested load_digits (1797 samples, 64 features, 10 classes) which is perfect for multiclass
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Checked dataset properties and confirmed it matches the paper's problem setting

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Help me write clean comments for my data loading and preprocessing code
full_llm_interaction_log.response_summary: The AI suggested inline comments for each preprocessing step: loading with return_X_y, StandardScaler normalization (noting that SVM performance is sensitive to feature scaling), and train_test_split with stratification to maintain class balance.
full_llm_interaction_log.what_it_helped_with: Got cleaner inline comments. I wrote the actual preprocessing code (StandardScaler, train_test_split) myself
full_llm_interaction_log.verification_method: Standard sklearn preprocessing — I know how this works from labs
